questions.promptTex: y = 5 \cdot 3^x. \\ \text{This function exhibits:}
questions.explanation: Base $b = 3 > 1$ → exponential **growth**. Each step multiplies $y$ by $3$.

questions.promptTex: y = 100 \cdot (0.5)^x. \\ \text{This function exhibits:}
hints: Multiplying by a fraction shrinks values.
questions.explanation: Base $b = 0.5$ is between $0$ and $1$ → exponential **decay**. Values halve each step.

questions.promptTex: y = 4 \cdot 2^x. \\ \text{What is } y \text{ when } x = 0?
questions.explanation: $y(0) = 4 \cdot 2^0 = 4 \cdot 1 = 4$. The y-intercept is always $a$ — the starting value.

questions.promptTex: \text{A population doubles every year. If it starts at 50, what is it after 4 years?}
questions.explanation: $P(t) = 50 \cdot 2^t$. $P(4) = 50 \cdot 16 = 800$. Doubling four times: $50 \to 100 \to 200 \to 400 \to 800$.

questions.promptTex: \text{The natural exponential base } e \text{ is approximately:}
hints: Not π (3.14...) and not the golden ratio.
questions.explanation: $e \approx 2.71828$. It's the limit of $(1 + \frac{1}{n})^n$ as $n \to \infty$ and the natural base for continuously compounded growth.

questions.promptTex: \text{Compound interest: \$2000 invested at 5\% annual rate, compounded yearly,} \\ \text{for 10 years. Final balance (round to nearest dollar)?}
\text{Half-life of a radioactive substance is } 5 \text{ years.} \\ \text{Starting with } 100 \text{ g, how much remains after } 15 \text{ years?}
steps: After each half-life, the amount halves.

questions.promptTex: \text{A bacterial culture doubles every 3 hours. Starting with } 200 \text{ bacteria,} \\ \text{how many after 12 hours?}
\text{Continuous compounding: \$1000 at 4\% rate for 5 years.} \\ \text{Use } A = Pe^{rt}, e \approx 2.71828. \text{ Find } A \text{ (nearest dollar).}
\text{A population decays at 10\% per year. After 5 years, what fraction remains?}
hints: 10% decay → 90% remains each year. Decay factor 0.9.
questions.explanation: Decay factor $= 1 - 0.10 = 0.9$. After 5 years: $(0.9)^5 \approx 0.5905$ ≈ 59% remains.
steps: Decay rate $10\%$ → keeps $90\%$ each year. Decay factor $= 0.9$.

questions.promptTex: \text{An investment doubles every 7 years.} \\ \text{Starting with \$5000, what is the value after 21 years?}
\text{Which is a } \textbf{decay} \text{ function?}
questions.explanation: $y = 100(0.7)^x$ has base $0.7 < 1$ → exponential decay.

questions.promptTex: \text{Solve for all real } x: \quad 4^x - 5 \cdot 2^x + 4 = 0. \\ \text{(Enter the SUM of the solutions.)}
hints: Notice $4^x = (2^x)^2$. Substitute $u = 2^x$ to get a quadratic in $u$.
questions.explanation: Let $u = 2^x$. Then $4^x = u^2$, so $u^2 - 5u + 4 = 0 \Rightarrow (u-1)(u-4) = 0 \Rightarrow u = 1$ or $u = 4$. Back-substitute: $2^x = 1 \Rightarrow x = 0$; $2^x = 4 \Rightarrow x = 2$. Sum $= 0 + 2 = 2$.

questions.promptTex: \text{Solve for } x: \quad 9^x + 3^{x+1} - 4 = 0.
hints: Substitute $u = 3^x$: $u^2 + 3u - 4 = 0$. Reject the negative root (an exponential is always positive).
questions.explanation: Rewrite: $9^x = (3^x)^2 = u^2$ and $3^{x+1} = 3 \cdot 3^x = 3u$, where $u = 3^x$. So $u^2 + 3u - 4 = 0 \Rightarrow (u+4)(u-1) = 0$. Reject $u = -4$ (since $3^x > 0$ for all real $x$). So $3^x = 1 \Rightarrow x = 0$.

questions.promptTex: \text{Solve } e^{2x} - 7e^x + 12 = 0. \\ \text{(Enter the SUM of the two solutions, in decimal form, accurate to 2 places.)}
hints: Substitute $u = e^x$ to get a quadratic.
questions.explanation: Let $u = e^x$: $u^2 - 7u + 12 = 0 \Rightarrow (u - 3)(u - 4) = 0 \Rightarrow u = 3$ or $u = 4$. So $x = \ln 3$ or $x = \ln 4$. Sum $= \ln 3 + \ln 4 = \ln(3 \cdot 4) = \ln 12 \approx 2.485$. (The product rule of logs collapses the sum into a single $\ln$.)

questions.promptTex: \text{An investment grows by } 5\% \text{ each year.} \\ \text{What is the smallest whole number of years for which the value MORE THAN doubles?}
questions.explanation: Solve $1.05^t > 2$. Take $\log_{10}$: $t \log 1.05 > \log 2 \Rightarrow t > \dfrac{\log 2}{\log 1.05} \approx \dfrac{0.30103}{0.02119} \approx 14.21$. Smallest integer strictly above $14.21$ is $\boxed{15}$. (Rule-of-72 estimate: $72/5 = 14.4$ — close.)
steps: Round UP to the next integer: $t = 15$ years.

questions.promptTex: \text{Carbon-14 has a half-life of } 5730 \text{ years.} \\ \text{A fossil contains } 25\% \text{ of the original C-14. How old is it (in years)?}
questions.explanation: $25\% = 1/4 = (1/2)^2$ — exactly two half-lives have elapsed. Time $= 2 \cdot 5730 = 11{,}460$ years. (Equivalently, solving $0.25 = (1/2)^{t/5730}$ gives $t/5730 = 2$, so $t = 11{,}460$.)

questions.promptTex: \text{Richter magnitude } M = \log_{10}(I/I_0). \\ \text{How many times stronger (in wave amplitude) is a magnitude } 7.0 \text{ earthquake than a } 4.0?
hints: A 1-unit difference in magnitude is a factor of $10$ in amplitude.
questions.explanation: From the definition, $I_1/I_2 = 10^{M_1 - M_2}$. With $M_1 = 7$, $M_2 = 4$: ratio $= 10^{7 - 4} = 10^3 = 1000$. (Most students assume Richter is linear and answer 'about twice as strong' — the logarithmic scale is the whole point.)

questions.promptTex: \text{Lemon juice has pH } 2 \text{ and black coffee has pH } 5. \\ \mathrm{pH} = -\log_{10}[\mathrm{H}^+]. \\ \text{How many times more acidic (in } [\mathrm{H}^+] \text{) is the lemon juice?}
hints: Lower pH means more acidic. Ratio $= 10^{\mathrm{pH}_{\text{coffee}} - \mathrm{pH}_{\text{lemon}}}$.
questions.explanation: $[\mathrm{H}^+] = 10^{-\mathrm{pH}}$. Ratio $= 10^{-2} / 10^{-5} = 10^{5 - 2} = 10^3 = 1000$. So lemon juice has $1000$ times the $\mathrm{H}^+$ concentration of coffee. Each 1-unit drop in pH is a $\times 10$ in acidity.

questions.promptTex: \text{Coffee at } 200°\mathrm{F} \text{ sits in a } 70°\mathrm{F} \text{ room and cools to } 150°\mathrm{F} \text{ after } 10 \text{ minutes.} \\ \text{Using } T(t) = 70 + 130 e^{-kt}, \text{ how many minutes (from when poured) until it reaches } 100°\mathrm{F}? \\ \text{(Round to the nearest minute.)}
hints: First find $k$ using the data point: $150 = 70 + 130 e^{-10k}$.
questions.explanation: Step 1: Find $k$. From $150 = 70 + 130 e^{-10k}$: $e^{-10k} = 80/130 \Rightarrow k = -\tfrac{1}{10} \ln(80/130) = \tfrac{1}{10}\ln(130/80) \approx 0.04855$. Step 2: Solve for $t$ when $T = 100$: $30 = 130 e^{-kt} \Rightarrow t = \tfrac{1}{k}\ln(130/30) \approx 1.4663 / 0.04855 \approx 30.2$ min.
steps: Use the 10-minute data point: $150 = 70 + 130 e^{-10k} \Rightarrow e^{-10k} = 80/130$.

questions.promptTex: \text{Solve for } x: \quad 2^{x+1} = 32.
questions.explanation: $32 = 2^5$, so $2^{x+1} = 2^5$. Equate exponents: $x + 1 = 5 \Rightarrow x = 4$. (Same-base trick — no logs needed.)

questions.promptTex: \text{Solve for } x: \quad 5^{2x} = 125.
questions.explanation: $125 = 5^3$, so $5^{2x} = 5^3 \Rightarrow 2x = 3 \Rightarrow x = 3/2 = 1.5$.

questions.promptTex: \text{Solve for } x: \quad e^{x} = 50. \\ \text{(Round to 3 decimal places.)}
questions.explanation: $e^x = 50 \Rightarrow x = \ln 50 \approx 3.912$. ($\ln$ undoes $e$ — use whenever the base is $e$.)

questions.promptTex: \text{An investment grows at } 4\% \text{ per year, compounded annually.} \\ \text{After how many full years will the investment first be at least TRIPLE?}
questions.explanation: Solve $1.04^t \geq 3$. Take $\log$: $t \log 1.04 \geq \log 3 \Rightarrow t \geq \dfrac{\log 3}{\log 1.04} \approx \dfrac{0.4771}{0.01703} \approx 28.01$. Smallest integer: $t = 29$. (Check: $1.04^{29} \approx 3.12$ — first year past triple.)

questions.promptTex: \text{A bacterial colony grows from } 200 \text{ to } 1800 \text{ in 5 hours.} \\ \text{Assuming } P(t) = 200 \cdot b^t, \text{ find the growth factor } b. \\ \text{(Round to 3 decimals.)}
questions.explanation: $200 \cdot b^5 = 1800 \Rightarrow b^5 = 9 \Rightarrow b = 9^{1/5} \approx 1.552$. (So the colony grows by about $55.2\%$ each hour.)

questions.promptTex: \text{A car worth } \$30{,}000 \text{ depreciates by } 15\% \text{ each year.} \\ \text{What is its value after 5 years (to the nearest dollar)?}
hints: Each year, the car retains $85\%$ of its prior value: $V = 30000 \cdot (0.85)^t$.
questions.explanation: Decay model: $V(t) = 30000 \cdot (0.85)^t$. After 5 years: $V(5) = 30000 \cdot 0.85^5 \approx 30000 \cdot 0.4437 \approx \$13{,}311$.

questions.promptTex: \text{A radioactive isotope has a half-life of } 8 \text{ days.} \\ \text{Starting with } 80 \text{ mg, how much remains after } 20 \text{ days? (Round to 1 decimal.)}
questions.explanation: Half-lives elapsed: $20/8 = 2.5$. Remaining: $80 \cdot (1/2)^{2.5} = 80 / 2^{2.5} = 80 / (4\sqrt{2}) \approx 80 / 5.657 \approx 14.1$ mg.

questions.promptTex: \text{Solve for } r \text{ to the nearest ten-thousandth: } \quad 7 e^{r+1} = 52.3.
hints: First isolate $e^{r+1}$: divide both sides by $7$.
questions.explanation: $7 e^{r+1} = 52.3 \Rightarrow e^{r+1} = 52.3/7 \approx 7.4714 \Rightarrow r + 1 = \ln(7.4714) \approx 2.0111 \Rightarrow r \approx 1.0111$.

questions.promptTex: \text{Solve for } x \text{ to the nearest ten-thousandth: } \quad -2 e^{6x} = -69.8.
questions.explanation: $-2 e^{6x} = -69.8 \Rightarrow e^{6x} = 34.9 \Rightarrow 6x = \ln(34.9) \approx 3.5525 \Rightarrow x \approx 0.5921$.

questions.promptTex: \text{An investment earns } 3.7\% \text{ interest compounded continuously.} \\ \text{How many years until it doubles? (Use } A = P e^{rt}; \text{ round to 4 decimals.)}
hints: Doubling means $A = 2P$, so $2P = P e^{0.037 t} \Rightarrow 2 = e^{0.037t}$.
questions.explanation: $2P = P e^{0.037 t} \Rightarrow 2 = e^{0.037 t} \Rightarrow 0.037 t = \ln 2 \Rightarrow t = \dfrac{\ln 2}{0.037} \approx \dfrac{0.6931}{0.037} \approx 18.7337$ years. (Sanity check via the rule of 72: $72 / 3.7 \approx 19.5$ — close.)

questions.promptTex: \text{An initial investment of } \$10{,}000 \text{ earns } 9.3\% \text{ interest compounded continuously.} \\ \text{How much is in the account after } 12 \text{ years? (Round to the nearest cent.)}
questions.explanation: $A = P e^{rt} = 10{,}000 \cdot e^{0.093 \cdot 12} = 10{,}000 \cdot e^{1.116} \approx 10{,}000 \cdot 3.05262 \approx \$30{,}526.19$. (After 12 years the account has roughly tripled.)
